solomon_islands_coalition_instability.claim: Solomon Islands coalition politics are notoriously fluid; the leader of a no-confidence motion does not always become PM, as coalition negotiations can produce compromise candidates.

provenance.definition: Solomon Islands coalition politics are notoriously fluid; the leader of a no-confidence motion does not always become PM, as coalition negotiations can produce compromise candidates.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 2 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: In 2019, after a no-confidence motion against Prime Minister Rick Hou, the opposition coalition elected Manasseh Sogavare as PM, not the motion's leader.
cases.basis_quote: Manasseh Sogavare was elected prime minister by parliament on 24 April 2019, replacing Rick Hou, after a no-confidence vote.

cases.description: In 2014, after the general election, a coalition led by the Democratic Alliance Party elected Manasseh Sogavare as PM, bypassing other candidates.
cases.basis_quote: Manasseh Sogavare was elected prime minister by parliament on 9 December 2014, heading a coalition government.

china_taiwan_diplomatic_rivalry.claim: Solomon Islands' shift to China in 2019 and subsequent tensions with Taiwan create external pressure on leadership choices, affecting coalition stability.

provenance.definition: Solomon Islands' shift to China in 2019 and subsequent tensions with Taiwan create external pressure on leadership choices, affecting coalition stability.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: In 2019, Prime Minister Manasseh Sogavare switched diplomatic recognition from Taiwan to China, leading to internal political turmoil and a no-confidence vote.
cases.basis_quote: Solomon Islands switches diplomatic recognition from Taiwan to China, prompting political crisis.

economic_dependence_on_aid.claim: Solomon Islands relies heavily on foreign aid, particularly from China and Australia, which influences political alignments and coalition formation.

provenance.definition: Solomon Islands relies heavily on foreign aid, particularly from China and Australia, which influences political alignments and coalition formation.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: In 2021, the Solomon Islands government accepted a security pact with China, reflecting aid-driven alignment.
cases.basis_quote: Solomon Islands signs security pact with China, deepening ties amid aid dependence.

matthew_wale.quantity: Matthew Wale is elected Prime Minister

provenance.definition: Matthew Wale is formally elected or appointed as Prime Minister of Solomon Islands and sworn in by the Governor-General.
provenance.inclusion_criteria: Official announcement by Solomon Islands Government or credible news reporting (ABC News Australia, RNZ, etc.) that Matthew Wale has been elected and sworn in as Prime Minister.
provenance.exclusion_criteria: Any other individual elected; no election by August 1, 2026; Wale not sworn in.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: Matthew Wale was elected as Leader of the Opposition in 2019, but never became PM in prior no-confidence attempts.
cases.basis_quote: Matthew Wale elected as Leader of the Opposition, but not Prime Minister.

frederick_kologeto.quantity: Frederick Kologeto is elected Prime Minister

provenance.definition: Frederick Kologeto is formally elected or appointed as Prime Minister and sworn in.
provenance.inclusion_criteria: Official announcement or credible reporting that Frederick Kologeto has been elected and sworn in as Prime Minister.
provenance.exclusion_criteria: Any other individual elected; no election by deadline.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: Frederick Kologeto was a candidate for Prime Minister in 2019 but lost to Manasseh Sogavare.
cases.basis_quote: Frederick Kologeto was a candidate for prime minister but was not elected.

jeremiah_manele.quantity: Jeremiah Manele is re-elected Prime Minister

provenance.definition: Jeremiah Manele is re-elected as Prime Minister after the no-confidence vote.
provenance.inclusion_criteria: Official announcement or credible reporting that Jeremiah Manele has been re-elected and sworn in as Prime Minister.
provenance.exclusion_criteria: Any other individual elected; no election by deadline.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: Jeremiah Manele was elected Prime Minister in 2024.
cases.basis_quote: Jeremiah Manele elected prime minister in 2024.

cases.description: Jeremiah Manele ousted via no-confidence on May 7, 2026.
cases.basis_quote: Jeremiah Manele ousted via no-confidence vote.

outcome_reference_class.quantity: Matthew Wale is elected Prime Minister

provenance.definition: Matthew Wale is formally elected or appointed as Prime Minister of Solomon Islands and sworn in by the Governor-General on or after May 7, 2026.
provenance.inclusion_criteria: Official announcement by Solomon Islands Government or credible news reporting (ABC News Australia, RNZ, etc.) that Matthew Wale has been elected and sworn in as Prime Minister.
provenance.exclusion_criteria: Any other individual elected; no election by August 1, 2026; Wale not sworn in.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 2 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: In 2019, after a no-confidence vote, Manasseh Sogavare was elected PM, not the opposition leader.
cases.basis_quote: Manasseh Sogavare elected prime minister after no-confidence vote, not opposition leader.

cases.description: In 2014, after general election, Manasseh Sogavare elected PM from coalition.
cases.basis_quote: Manasseh Sogavare elected prime minister from coalition.

parliament_of_solomon_islands.consequence_of_nonparticipation: If no new PM elected by August 1, 2026, the question resolves No.
